sampleQuestions.stem: Context-free languages and regular languages are both closed under the operation(s) of: A. Union B. Intersection C. Concatenation D. Complementation

Which of the following three statements are true? Prove your answer. i. The union of two recursive languages is recursive. ii. The language {0^n | n is a prime} is not regular. iii. Regular languages are closed under inf

Which of the following is true? A. The complement of a recursive language is recursive B. The complement of a recursively enumerable language is recursively enumerable C. The complement of a recursive language is either
FORTRAN is a: A. Regular language. B. Context-free language. C. Context-sensitive language. D. None of the above.
What is the type of the language L, where L = {0^n | 0 < n, n is a prime number}? (Identify which class in the Chomsky hierarchy L belongs to, and justify.)
Show that the Turing machines, which have a read-only input tape and a constant-size work tape, recognize precisely the class of regular languages.
